hadith_entries.text:
676. Narrated Abu-Huraira: 'Allah's Messenger
"Observing fasting is a shield (or a screen or a shelter from
Hellfire). So a person observing the fast should avoid sexual
acts and should not behave foolishly and impudently, and if
somebody quarrels with him or abuses him, he should say to
him twice: 'I am observing fasting." The Prophet added:
"By Him in Whose Hands my soul is, the smell coming from
the mouth of a person observing fasting is better with Allah
than the smell of musk. (Allah says about the fasting
person): 'He has left his food, drink, and desires for My sake.
Fasting is for Me. So I will reward (the fasting person) for it
and the reward of good deeds is multiplied ten times."
(Bukhari)
البخاري.

677. Narrated Abu-Huraira: 'The Prophet said: "Whoever
does not give up lying speech (false statements) and acting on
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
those lies and evil actions, etc., Allah is not in need of his
leaving his food and drink (Allah will not accept his fasting)."
(Bukhari)
